items.title: The Simple Beauty of For Loops
items.author: Sam's Dev Lab
items.content:
Ahh, the for loop. A highly important tool for the programmer.

I've been learning JavaScript for the past eight months and I have really come to enjoy using for loops.

The first time I used a for loop to create multiple DOM elements at once, I felt like a wizard and a genius 😎. Call me "Gandalf", bruh, 'cause I'm out here castin' spells.

Looping through arrays of data and creating multiple dynamic elements in the DOM are just a couple of ways I have used "for loops" since I began my coding journey.

There are many different variations of the for loop - "for...of", "for...in", "forEach". But today, we'll just focus on the regular ol' for loop.

What Even a For Loop, Though?

Stealing this simple definition from MDN Web Docs, "Loops offer a quick and easy way to do something repeatedly."

It's that simple. Though, if you're brand new to programming, that definition may not suffice without adding a bit of context.

So, let's first explore the structure of the for loop and break it down piece by piece.

for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
console.log(i);
}

^ is a for loop. Let's dive into what the hell is going on here.

We'll quickly break down what's happening in this simple for loop then further explore each individual section.

  1. Declare a counter to begin at 0.
  2. Write a condition to stop the loop (the loop stops before reaching 10).
  3. Running a block of code for each iteration (console.log(i), in this case).
  4. Increment the counter after the code block runs (move from 0 to 1).

Let's break it down step-by-step:

The loop allows us to through a set number of values. Therefore, it's common practice to use "i" as our variable name - "i" for "iteration" - get it?! Pretty intuitive, yah?

Also, we utilize a "let" variable, rather than "const", because the value of the of "i" will be reassigned each time we iterate through the loop. As you may already know, the value of a "let" variable can change, while the value of a "const" variable cannot.

That said, within the first part of the loop, we set the value of our variable "i", to "0". In programming, we generally begin counting from "0" rather than "1", though you can set this value to anything you like.

So is now our starting variable.

So far, so good. Onto the next section.

Declare an Action Within Our Code Block

We have created a code block with our curly braces {}.

Anything within this code block will run at the beginning of the for loop and again each time we iterate to the next value.

For this particular code block, we are simply logging the current value of "i" to our console.

Therefore, when "i = 0", our code block will log the value of "i" to the console. Meaning "0" will be logged to the console.

Set a Condition to Stop Loop - You Don't Wanna Overdo It

Remember grade school math? means the value of 'i' will never make it to '10'. If we did , our count make it to '10' because we included '10' by using our "=" sign. "i is less than, or equal to, '10'."

For the purposes of this tutorial, however, "i" is less than 10.

This part of the for loop sets the maximum value that we'll be aiming to reach.

Think of this as our for loop's "limit". If we don't set a limit, this will result in what's known as an "infinite loop". An infinite loop will increment indefinitely and thereby crash our program.

The loop would run "to infinity and beyond!" Great for space exploration, not so great for the programmer.

Increment after Code Block Execution

This part of the for loop allows the actual counting to take place. It says "Once I run your code block for 'let i = 0', I'm going to bump your variable up to 'let i = 1' and run the code block all over again."

It's dope like that. We just sit back and watch the magic take place.

Okay, So What Are the Results? 

for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++){
console.log(i)
}

This results in the following output to our console:

Nice! We set parameters for our for loop and printed some results in the matter of milliseconds! Pretty cool.

If our for loop were a human, he might say: "Well i = 0, so I better use that to complete the action in the code block. Ah, looks like we're going to log the value of "i" to the console. Okay, I logged that, what's next? Ah, we're incrementing to let i = 1? Okay, looks like 1 needs to be logged to the console..." and so on, until we reach the maximum we set for the for loop - in this case, "9".

Implications of the For Loop

We counted 10 values. Fantastic. Okay, what else can we do with a for loop? There are many use cases, which I won't fully explore in this quick introduction.

But let's just say we need 30 different HTML divs, for example.

Yes, I could painstakingly type out 30 different divs in my HTML. But this ain't the 1500s, and I'm not an ancient monk working by candlelight. I'm way too lazy to do something like that.

Instead, we use a for loop and instantly create 30 divs at the drop of a hat.

// Grab the main div container from the DOM
const mainContainer = document.querySelector("#main-container");

/* 
Loop through numbers 0 -> 29 (remember, less than 30 so 30 doesn't get counted)
*/

for (let i = 0; i < 30; i++) {
  const innerDiv = document.createElement("div");
  innerDiv.classList.add("inner-div");

// Append new innerDivs to the mainContainer
  mainContainer.appendChild(innerDiv);
}

I won't go into details of appending the DOM in this blog post - just know that I dynamically created 30 different divs in the DOM using this little block of code. Pretty sweet, huh?

In closing, learning the for loop seemed so difficult to me mere months ago. Now, I use it constantly.

In future blog posts, I plan on delving into the many different loops variations. After all, loops serve as a major foundation of programming.

For now, just know that they are simpler than they appear at first glance and are quite useful.

items.title: Isolamento no TDAH: Como enfrentar a solidão e melhorar sua vida
items.author: Geraldo Batista
items.content:
O isolamento associado ao transtorno de déficit de atenção e hiperatividade (TDAH) é uma experiência muitas vezes silenciosa e desafiadora para quem vive com essa condição. Apesar de ser uma origem frequente de dificuldades sociais e emocionais, esse isolamento muitas vezes passa despercebido, contribuindo para sentimentos de solidão, frustração e até mesmo baixa autoestima. Crianças, adolescentes e adultos com TDAH podem sentir-se excluídos ou incompreendidos pelas suas próprias limitações, dificultando a formação de conexões duradouras e saudáveis. Além disso, o estigma social e as dificuldades de comunicação intensificam esse isolamento, criando um ciclo vicioso que prejudica o bem-estar e a qualidade de vida. Compreender a magnitude do isolamento no contexto do TDAH é fundamental para promover estratégias de suporte mais eficazes, promovendo inclusão, empatia e ações que fortalecem vínculos sociais e emocionais, proporcionando uma vida mais plena para quem enfrenta esse desafio.

Impacto emocional do isolamento no TDAH O isolamento associado ao transtorno de déficit de atenção e hiperatividade (TDAH) não afeta apenas o aspecto social, mas também tem consequências emocionais profundas. Crianças, adolescentes e adultos que enfrentam o isolamento tdah frequentemente desenvolvem sentimentos de solidão, frustração e baixa autoestima. Essa sensação de estar à margem do grupo pode criar uma espécie de ciclo vicioso, no qual a pessoa se sente incapaz de estabelecer conexões duradouras devido à própria dificuldade de lidar com suas limitações. Muitos indivíduos relatam que esse isolamento aumenta seu nível de ansiedade e contribui para problemas emocionais, como transtornos depressivos. É essencial compreender que o suporte emocional e o reconhecimento das experiências subjetivas de quem vive com TDAH são fundamentais para mitigar esses efeitos. Estratégias de intervenção que incluam terapia emocional, por exemplo, podem ajudar a fortalecer a autoestima e a lidar melhor com as emoções, criando pontes que reduzam o impacto do isolamento tdah na saúde mental. Reconhecer esse impacto emocional é o primeiro passo para promover mudançаs significativas na vida dessas pessoas.

Fatores sociais que contribuem para o isolamento tdah As dificuldades de comunicação e compreensão social são fatores concomitantes ao isolamento tdah. Crianças e adolescentes com TDAH muitas vezes têm dificuldades em manter o foco em conversas, interpretar sinais sociais ou regular suas emoções, o que pode gerar conflitos ou mal-entendidos com colegas. Além disso, o estigma social ligado ao transtorno alimenta uma percepção negativa, levando à exclusão ou ao bullying. tdah como ajudar na escola criam uma barreira que dificulta a formação de vínculos duradouros e aumenta o sentimento de isolamento. É comum também que familiares e professores, apesar da boa intenção, não estejam preparados para lidar com as particularidades do TDAH, o que reforça a sensação de incompreensão. adolescentes com tdah na escola dificuldades aumentem o isolamento tdah, é fundamental promover ações educativas, programas de conscientização e treinamentos que capacitem as pessoas ao redor. A inclusão social passa pelo entendimento e aceitação, fatores essenciais para transformar o isolamento em conexão e empatia.

O papel da comunicação e do suporte familiar A comunicação aberta e o suporte familiar desempenham papéis cruciais na redução do isolamento tdah. Muitas vezes, familiares de pessoas com TDAH enfrentam seus próprios desafios, como frustração ou medo de não compreender as dificuldades enfrentadas pelo ente querido. Entretanto, uma abordagem empática, baseada na escuta ativa e no incentivo ao diálogo, pode fazer a diferença. crianças com tdah na escola criar um ambiente de confiança, a família consegue acolher as emoções, reforçar o sentimento de pertencimento e oferecer o suporte necessário para que o indivíduo conquiste suas conexões sociais. Um exemplo prático é a implementação de rotinas familiares estruturadas, que oferecem previsibilidade e segurança, além de espaços onde a pessoa possa expressar suas dúvidas e inseguranças sem julgamento. Nesse contexto, o apoio familiar é uma estratégia eficaz para amenizar o isolamento tdah, promovendo um ambiente mais inclusivo e acolhedor que favoreça o desenvolvimento social e emocional.

Intervenções educacionais e a inclusão social A escola desempenha papel fundamental na minimização do isolamento tdah. Crianças com TDAH podem sentir-se excluídas devido às suas dificuldades de atenção, leia o post completo hiperatividade, que podem causar dificuldades na convivência com colegas ou na adaptação às rotinas escolares. Programas de intervenção educacional aliados à formação de professores para lidar com o transtorno podem favorecer uma inclusão mais efetiva. Exemplos práticos incluem salas de aula com metodologias diferenciadas, acompanhamento individualizado e atividades que promovam a cooperação. Essas ações contribuem para que o aluno se sinta acolhido e compreendido, reduzindo sensações de isolamento. A inclusão escolar de crianças e adolescentes com TDAH é uma estratégia que vai além do ambiente acadêmico, promovendo a integração social, fundamental para prevenir o isolamento tdah. Quanto mais o contexto escolar se tornar um espaço de aceitação, menor será o risco de o indivíduo se sentir excluído ou invisível.

Estratégias de suporte emocional e terapia Para enfrentar o isolamento tdah, profissionais de saúde mental recomendam a implementação de estratégias de suporte emocional e terapia. estresse por trabalho -comportamentais, por exemplo, podem ajudar o indivíduo a desenvolver habilidades sociais, a administrar suas emoções e a melhorar sua autoestima. Dentro do tratamento, é possível trabalhar também a percepção que a pessoa tem de si mesma, ajudando a superar o sentimento de inadequação que muitas vezes é alimentado pelo isolamento. Além disso, grupos de apoio, onde pessoas com TDAH compartilham experiências, desempenham papel importante na promoção de conexões autênticas, combatendo a solidão. Um exemplo prático seria um adulto que participa de encontros de apoio, onde consegue entender que não está só em suas dificuldades, o que diminui sua sensação de isolamento. 10 sintomas de tdah na escola ações fortalecem o sentimento de pertencimento e promovem um ciclo mais saudável, onde o suporte emocional oferece uma alternativa eficaz ao isolamento social e emocional.

Conclusão: promovendo inclusão e bem-estar O isolamento tdah é uma questão complexa que envolve fatores emocionais, sociais e ambientais. Sua compreensão detalhada permite o desenvolvimento de estratégias mais eficazes para promover inclusão, empatia e apoio. Investir em educação, suporte familiar, intervenção escolar e terapia emocional são caminhos essenciais para reduzir esse isolamento. As ações que fortalecem vínculos sociais e estimulam a compreensão do transtorno podem transformar a experiência de quem vive com TDAH, tornando-a mais plena e integrada na sociedade. Quanto maior a conscientização e o esforço coletivo para criar ambientes acolhedores, menor será o impacto do isolamento tdah, promovendo uma vida mais equilibrada e satisfatória para esses indivíduos. Assim, priorizar a inclusão e o bem-estar é fundamental para transformar o desafio do isolamento em uma oportunidade de crescimento e conexão. 10 sintomas de tdah na escola

items.title: Linux in Action: Mastering Group Account Management & Operations
items.author: DhavalThakar97
items.content:
In Linux, user management doesn't end at the individual level—group management is equally vital for maintaining a secure, efficient, and organized system. Groups allow system administrators to streamline permissions, collaborate within teams, and enforce consistent access policies across users.

This article dives deep into Linux group account management. We'll explore group types, system files involved, essential commands, and real-world operational tips that apply from small teams to enterprise-grade environments.

  • What Are Groups in Linux?
  • Essential Group Management Commands
  • Real-World Notes and Operational Considerations

What Are Groups in Linux?

A group is a logical collection of user accounts. Groups make permission management easier by allowing shared access to files, directories, and system resources among multiple users.

  • Automatically created when a new user is added.
  • Each user is assigned a default primary group.
  • When the user is deleted, the primary group is removed as well.
  • Created manually by a superuser.
  • Users can be added or removed flexibly.
  • A user may belong to multiple secondary groups simultaneously.

Grouping users is essential for organizing permissions in collaborative environments, such as dev teams, support staff, or project-based access roles.

Two critical system files manage group-related data:

  • Defines group names, GIDs (Group IDs), and members.
  • Readable by all users but modifiable only by root.
  • Stores secure group administration data (group passwords, admins).
  • Accessible only by privileged users.

Use to inspect specific group properties:

devteam /etc/group
devteam /etc/gshadow

Essential Group Management Commands

Creates a new secondary group named .

Verifies creation, members, and GID.

Changes the GID of an existing group.

  • Add Single Member to Group:
  • Remove Single Member from Group:

Removes user from .

  • Add/Remove Multiple Members:
gpasswd  bob,charlie,dan devteam

🛑 This overwrites all existing group members. Always include both new and existing members. Also removes the admin, though they retain implicit privileges.

Sets as the group admin.

Removes all admin users from the group.

Displays admin and password info.

Removes the group from the system.

Real-World Notes and Operational Considerations

✅ Automate group operations using tools like Ansible or shell scripts. as it replaces all members.

✅ Maintain consistent naming conventions like

, .

✅ Log all group operations in enterprise setups for audit and compliance.

Mastering group management is essential for scalable Linux administration. From simplifying permissions to maintaining audit compliance, groups provide a powerful mechanism for managing user access efficiently and securely.

Stay consistent, automate where possible, and always verify changes.

items.title: Frontend'in Derinliklerinde: Kodun Ötesinde Tasarım
items.author: Yunus Emre Mert
items.content:
Yaz tatili, yalnızca güneşli günlerin ve dinlendirici manzaraların keyfini çıkarmakla kalmaz, aynı zamanda yeni beceriler öğrenmek ve yaratıcılığımızı keşfetmek için de harika bir fırsattır. Özellikle web geliştiricileri için, ön uç geliştirme becerilerini geliştirmek ve kullanıcı deneyimini yeni seviyelere taşımak için mükemmel bir zaman. Bu yaz, "Frontend'in Derinliklerinde: Kodun Ötesinde Tasarım" başlıklı bir yolculuğa çıkıyoruz.

Web geliştirme, yalnızca kod satırlarından ibaret değildir; tasarım, kullanıcı deneyimi ve estetik de önemli bir rol oynar. Ön uç, web sitesinin veya uygulamanın kullanıcılarla etkileşimde bulunduğu yerdir ve dikkatli bir tasarım ve uygulama, kullanıcının deneyimini tamamen değiştirebilir. Bu yazıda, ön uç geliştirme sürecinin kodun ötesine geçen yönlerini keşfederek, görsel tasarımın inceliklerini, kullanıcı deneyimi ilkelerini ve ön uç geliştiricilerin tasarım becerilerini nasıl geliştirebileceklerini inceleyeceğiz.

Ön Uç Tasarımın Temelleri

Ön uç tasarım, web sitesinin veya uygulamanın görsel yönü ve kullanıcı arayüzüyle ilgilidir. Kullanıcı deneyimini şekillendiren ve kullanıcıların platformla etkileşim şeklini etkileyen temel bir unsurdur. Ön uç tasarımın bazı temel unsurları şunlardır:

  1. : Yazı tipleri, yazı stilleri, yazı boyutları ve satır aralıkları gibi metinlerin görsel sunumu.

  2. : Renk şemaları, kontrast ve renk psikolojisi, duyguları ve eylemleri yönlendirmede güçlü araçlardır.

  3. : Önemli öğeleri vurgulamak ve kullanıcıların dikkatini yönlendirmek için görsel hiyerarşi oluşturulması.

  4. Kullanıcı Arayüzü Tasarımı: Düğmeler, menüler, formlar ve etkileşimli öğeler gibi kullanıcı arayüzü bileşenlerinin tasarımı ve yerleşimi.

  5. Kullanıcı Deneyimi İlkeleri: Kullanıcı dostu bir deneyim oluşturmak için kullanılabilirlik, erişilebilirlik ve kullanıcı yolculuğu gibi kavramlar.

Örneğin, bir e-ticaret web sitesi düşünün. Ürün görüntülerini, açıklamalarını ve fiyatlarını sunan bir ana sayfa düşünün. Tipografi, ürün başlıklarını ve açıklamalarını net ve okunaklı hale getirmek için dikkatli bir şekilde seçilebilir. Renk, markanın kimliğini yansıtabilir ve "Satın Al" düğmeleri dikkat çekici bir renkte tasarlanarak görsel hiyerarşiyi güçlendirebilir. Kullanıcı arayüzü tasarımı, kullanıcı dostu bir alışveriş deneyimi sağlamak için sezgisel ve kolay anlaşılır olmalıdır.

React ile Tasarım Uygulama

React, popüler bir JavaScript kütüphanesi, ön uç geliştiricilerin tasarımlarını hayata geçirmelerine olanak tanır. React bileşenleri, tasarım ilkelerini kodla birleştirmek için mükemmel bir yol sunar.

Diyelim ki basit bir "Hakkımızda" sayfası tasarlıyoruz. Sayfa, şirketin vizyonunu ve misyonunu içeren bir başlık, bazı tanıtıcı metinler ve bir "İletişim" düğmesi içerecek. React kullanarak bu tasarımı uygulayabiliriz:

Hakkımızda
        Vizyonumuz, müşterilerimize en yenilikçi ve kullanıcı dostu deneyimleri sunmaktır.
      
        Misyonumuz, tutku ve uzmanlıkla sürükleyici teknolojiler yaratmaktır.
      İletişim

Bu kodda, adlı bir React işlevsel bileşeni tanımlıyoruz. İşlev, sayfanın içeriğini temsil eden JSX kodunu döndürür. , , ve gibi HTML etiketlerini kullanıyoruz ve özniteliğiyle özel stil sınıfları uyguluyoruz.

Stil sınıflarını tanımlamak için ayrı bir CSS dosyası () kullanıyoruz:

Bu CSS stilleri, sayfanın genel düzenini, yazı tiplerini, renkleri ve düğme stilini belirler.

Gerçek Dünya Uygulamaları

  • : Ürün görüntülerini, açıklamalarını ve kullanıcı yorumlarını sunan görsel olarak çekici sayfalar oluşturmak için ön uç tasarım önemlidir.

  • Sosyal Medya Platformları: Kullanıcı profilleri, akışlar ve paylaşım seçenekleri gibi görsel öğeler, sosyal etkileşimi ve kullanıcı katılımını teşvik eder.

  • : Haber makaleleri, görsel tasarım yoluyla okunabilir ve erişilebilir hale getirilmelidir.

  • : Şirketin markasını ve değerlerini yansıtan profesyonel ve çekici tasarımlar, potansiyel müşteriler ve iş ortakları üzerinde güçlü bir etki yaratabilir.

Ön uç tasarım, web geliştirmenin ayrılmaz bir parçasıdır ve kodun ötesine geçerek kullanıcıların deneyimini şekillendirir. Bu yazıda, ön uç tasarımın temel unsurlarını keşfettik, React kullanarak tasarım uygulamayı öğrendik ve gerçek dünya senaryolarında tasarımın önemini vurguladık.

Ön uç geliştiriciler olarak, kodlama becerilerimizi görsel tasarım ilkeleriyle birleştirerek etkileyici ve kullanıcı dostu arayüzler oluşturabiliriz. Kullanıcı deneyimi ve estetik, web platformlarının başarısında kritik rol oynar ve bu becerileri geliştirmek, geliştiricilerin yeteneklerini yeni seviyelere taşıyabilir.

items.title: Never lose valuable error context in JavaScript
items.author: Amnish Singh Arora
items.content:
If you are a , you might have ran into error messages like:

  • Failed to extract text from the file

But what exactly led to failing of an entire high-level operation?

We don't know the because we didn't do a good enough job to preserve the error context, when throwing a specific error that is a of a more low level .

For years, JavaScript developers have struggled to preserve appropriate error context when throwing exceptions, as there was no in-built mechanism provided by the language to attach error cause, unlike other languages like Java, Python and C#.

This problem was solved by , when they finally added a cause property to their Error constructor in , which means you could now throw exceptions like so:

This means you now get to preserve the entire context of the original error that led to throwing of a high level exception (including its and ).

 1. The problem  2. The solution?  4. Configuration  5. Conclusion

This feature has been available in Node for a while now (added in 2022), and although it solves the of not having a standard way to preserve error context, it introduces a as its completely optional to attach the error cause to new exceptions.

What we need is a static analysis tool to enforce this as a coding convention — ensuring developers always attach available causes when throwing new errors.

While ESLint is the go-to tool for code quality in JavaScript, it doesn’t provide any built-in rule for this.

Introducing eslint-plugin-error-cause — an that flags any calls that omit the cause when inside a catch block.

Let's talk about how we can in our projects.

The very first thing you need to do is install the plugin and as dev dependencies in your project. This example uses pnpm, but you could use a package manager of your choice.

pnpm add -D eslint eslint-plugin-error-cause

Once installed, create an file at the root of your project.

There are to add this plugin to your configuration.

  • Use the in-built config, that enables rule with a severity level of .
  • Enable the rule manually with a severity level of your choice.

Setup scripts in your .

"scripts": {
    ...
    "lint": "eslint .",
    "lint:fix":  "pnpm lint --fix",
    ...
},

Use the script to report all the lint errors in your code. 🎊

> pnpm lint

3:10  error    'rootError' is defined but never used                                 no-unused-vars
4:5   warning  Include the original caught error as the `cause` of the custom error  error-cause/no-swallowed-error-cause

Use to fix any instances on .

Almost everyone uses VSCode extension to detect and fix lint errors in-place when coding.

If you don't have it setup, follow these steps:

Add this setting to your workspace/user . You could add/omit the JavaScript flavours based on your project needs.

"eslint.validate": [
  "javascript",
  "javascriptreact",
  "typescript",
  "typescriptreact"
  ],

Now you can detect and fix using VSCode's UI.

So that's all about how you could setup my new plugin in your projects, and about missing important error context when throwing custom exceptions!

This plugin is still very new and might not cover some edge-cases. I welcome any interested readers to visit the GitHub repo, open issues, send in pull requests, or even give it a and share with your connections if this helped you in any way 😁

Direct Associations: In standard Rails applications, the is opposite: an to foreign key columns enforcing the presence of a valid value unless you add the option .

So: if you decided to remove the database constraint from any of your foreign key columns in the migration file (e.g., change , to t.references :recipient, null: true ...), then you should also add the option to the corresponding association accessor.

So remember — if you’re ever in the situation of:

  • you’re trying to save a record
  • you’re doing the standard debugging technique of printing out zebra.errors.full_messages
  • you’re seeing an inexplicable validation error message saying that a foreign key column is blank
  • now you know where the validation is coming from: adds it automatically
  • so figure out why you’re not providing a valid foreign key (usually it is because the parent object failed to save for its own validation reasons)

Direct associations: Each , there should be an inverse . Check to make sure you have all of these inverse 1-N associations.

items.title: How To Introduce a New API Quickly Using Quarkus and ChatGPT
items.author: John Vester
items.content:
My last two articles (part 1 and part 2) focused on getting to market quickly using Java. The only difference was the build automation tool that I used for each example. This time, I want to step outside of my comfort zone and try something a little different.

I read about how Quarkus is a Kubernetes-native Java framework designed for building fast, lightweight microservices. What’s even better is that it is optimized for cloud environments, including features like fast startup times, low memory footprints, and support for both imperative and reactive programming models.

Like before, the key to success is how quickly we can go from idea to reality. In this case, let’s see how quickly I can establish a new API using Quarkus and deploy it to the cloud.

Leaning on ChatGPT for Assistance … Again

I started this series by asking ChatGPT to help create an OpenAPI specification for my Motivational Quotes service. This time, I’m asking ChatGPT how to get started with Quarkus for another instance of this service.

ChatGPT guides me through a series of steps, starting with using the Quarkus CLI, which I install locally with Homebrew.

brew quarkusio/tap/quarkus
quarkus 
3.19.4

I use the CLI to create the new project:

quarkus create app com.example.quotes:quotes-quarkus

The CLI responds with the following output:

Looking the newly published extensions registry.quarkus.io


applying codestarts...
✓ java
✓ maven
✓ quarkus
✓ config-properties
✓ tooling-dockerfiles
✓ tooling-maven-wrapper
✓ rest-codestart

SUCCESS] ✓  quarkus project has been successfully generated :
 /Users/johnvester/projects/jvc/quotes-quarkus

Navigate into this directory and get started: quarkus dev

We can see from scanning the root directory that Quarkus uses the Maven build automation tool by default:

quotes-quarkus 
total 96
drwxr-xr-x  11 johnvester    352 Mar 22 11:55 
drwxrwxrwx  91 root         2912 Mar 22 11:55 ..
@  1 johnvester    6148 Mar 22 11:55 .DS_Store

   1 johnvester    423 Mar 22 11:55 .gitignore
drwxr-xr-x   3 johnvester     96 Mar 22 11:55 .mvn

   1 johnvester  11172 Mar 22 11:55 mvnw
   1 johnvester   7697 Mar 22 11:55 mvnw.cmd
   1 johnvester   5003 Mar 22 11:55 pom.xml
drwxr-xr-x   5 johnvester    160 Mar 22 11:55 src

Like the OpenAPI specification in my first article, ChatGPT provides the necessary steps to get me to a point where I can be successful.

Developing My Quarkus Service

To avoid duplication of effort, I’ll continue to use the OpenAPI specification from my first article. 

4.0.0com.example.quotesquotes-quarkus1.0.0-SNAPSHOT3.14.01.18.36217.12.0UTF-8UTF-8quarkus-bomio.quarkus.platform3.19.4true3.5.2${quarkus.platform.group-id}${quarkus.platform.artifact-id}${quarkus.platform.version}pomimportio.quarkusquarkus-arcio.quarkusquarkus-resteasy-jacksonio.quarkusquarkus-smallrye-openapiorg.projectlomboklombok${lombok.version}providedio.quarkusquarkus-junit5testio.rest-assuredrest-assuredtestsrc/main/resources**/*${quarkus.platform.group-id}quarkus-maven-plugin${quarkus.platform.version}truebuildgenerate-codegenerate-code-testsnative-image-agentmaven-compiler-plugin${compiler-plugin.version}trueorg.projectlomboklombok1.18.36maven-surefire-plugin${surefire-plugin.version}org.jboss.logmanager.LogManager${maven.home}maven-failsafe-plugin${surefire-plugin.version}integration-testverify${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-runnerorg.jboss.logmanager.LogManager${maven.home}org.openapitoolsopenapi-generator-maven-plugin${openapi-generator-maven-plugin.version}quotes-apigenerate${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/META-INF/openapi.yamlcom.example.apicom.example.model${codegen.openapi.generated-sources-dir}jaxrs-specfalsefalsefalsefalsesrc/main/javatruefalsetruejava8falsecom.example.apinativenativefalsetrue

This time, we place the file into folder and create an file in the folder. The contents of the file are as follows:

Our file has the following contents:

Create the Business Logic

Like in my prior articles, we’ll use in-memory data for our motivational quotes. In a newly-created package, I add the class, which is very similar to what we’ve used so far:

Next, I add the following —which calls the —to a newly-created package.

Finally, I implement the created by our API-First approach. I create a class in a newly-created package with the following contents:

We can add a controller test by creating the in a newly-created test package:

We also add the integration test, which simply calls the test above:

Now we are ready to run our service.

Building and Running the Service

We use the following command to start a local instance of our API:

The command builds the project, runs the tests, and starts a local instance:

Listening transport dt_socket at address: 5005

We can validate that the service is working locally with the following curl command:

curl

When running in local dev mode, Quarkus provides a really nice developer UI:

The option provides both RESTful URLs and additional URLs to help ease the development process:

We can stop the local instance with .

Leveraging Heroku to Deploy the Service

Since I used Heroku for my prior articles, I wonder if support exists for Quarkus services. It turns out… it does! Going with Heroku helps me deploy my services quickly. I don’t lose time dealing with infrastructure concerns.

Like before, I need to allow for the service port to be overridden. In this case, we just need to update the following line in , as will be set by Heroku at runtime:

To match the Java version we’re using, we create a file in the root folder of the project. The file has one line:

Then, I create a in the same location for customizing the deployment behavior. This file also has one line:

web: java JAVA_OPTS  target/quarkus-app/quarkus-run.jar

Next we need to execute the command as a final step before we attempt to deploy to Heroku. Expanding the target folder in IntelliJ shows the file that is expected by the (above).

It’s time to deploy. With the Heroku CLI, I can deploy the service using a few simple commands. First, I authenticate the CLI and then create a new Heroku app.

heroku login 
heroku create

The CLI responds with the following response:

Creating app... , ⬢ murmuring-refuge-95709
https://murmuring-refuge-95709-a8582dfe9b2b.herokuapp.com/ | https://git.heroku.com/murmuring-refuge-95709.git

One last thing to do … push the code to Heroku, which deploys the service:

Switching back to Heroku Dashboard, we see our service has deployed successfully:

In addition to the JAR-based approach, Heroku supports deploying Quarkus services using a Docker or Podman container. Taking this approach provides complete control over its content, allowing deployment via a native executable running on GraalVM.

Motivational Quotes in Action

First, we retrieve the list of quotes:

curl

We can retrieve a single quote by its ID:

curl

We can retrieve a random motivational quote:

curl

We can view the Heroku Dashboard again to see our metrics after running these commands:

My readers may recall my personal mission statement, which I feel can apply to any IT professional:

“Focus your time on delivering features/functionality that extends the value of your intellectual property. Leverage frameworks, products, and services for everything else.” — J. Vester

In this article, I had to step outside my comfort zone and work with Quarkus for the very first time. I was able to ease the learning curve by leveraging ChatGPT. Once the project was created and I saw the build system being used was Maven, I was able to use my existing skills to get the API-First functionality in place.

From there, I was able to reach out to ChatGPT again to ask how I could convert my existing Spring Boot code to work with Quarkus. The ensuing response introduced me to the annotation used by Quarkus. Quarkus provided a CLI that helped me build and run the service locally, while also providing a Dev UI to ease the process to learn this new service option.

From a Heroku side, I was able to quickly deploy and validate my service using the same approach I have followed before. This saved me time in trying to figure out something new when my preference is to focus on making my service better.

For these reasons, ChatGPT, Quarkus, and Heroku all adhere to my mission statement. They helped me deploy the new API quickly and without a huge learning burden.

If you are interested in the source code for this article, it is available on GitLab.

items.title: React Mastery: Understanding `onChange={handleChange}` vs `onChange={(e) => handleChange(e)}`
items.author: Cristian Sifuentes
items.content:
React Mastery: Understanding vs onChange={(e) => handleChange(e)}

In modern React development, handling events is a foundational skill for building responsive, data-driven interfaces. Whether you’re crafting a dynamic form or building reusable components, knowing how to manage events efficiently can make your code simpler, more readable, and more performant.

Today, we’ll break down a deceptively simple difference that many React devs gloss over:

At first glance, both seem to do the same thing—pass the event to a handler. But there are that matter in real-world apps. Let's explore them together.

Imagine a typical React input form using TypeScript:

This setup works perfectly—and you could also write:

So, ?

Option 1:

This is the most efficient and direct way to bind an event handler.

  • You are passing the function reference directly.
  • React will call for you and inject the event as the first parameter.
  • No new function is created during rendering.
  • Better performance (no new function on every render).
  • Easier for React to optimize.
  • You cannot add additional arguments directly.
  • You must ensure accepts the correct event type.

Option 2: onChange={(e) => handleChange(e)}

This is a more flexible form using an inline arrow function.

  • A new function is created every time the component re-renders.
  • That new function calls your .
  • You can customize or transform before passing it in.
  • You can bind additional parameters.
  • Creates a new function on every render → slight performance cost.
  • Harder to optimize with , since props/functions are not stable.

Real Example with Debugging

✅ Both are in this simple use case.

But imagine if you added custom logic in the arrow function:

Now it becomes necessary to use the inline function.

Need extra logic before callingonChange={(e) => handleChange(e)}
Need to pass additional argumentsonChange={(e) => customFn(e, x)}
Performance optimization (frequent re-renders)

Both approaches are valid and commonly used in React. The key is to understand the :

  • For clean, efficient code: use .
  • For custom logic or parameterization: use .

By mastering these distinctions, you’ll write cleaner, faster, and more maintainable code. And that’s what React mastery is all about. 💪

If you’re using , avoid arrow functions in , , etc., unless wrapped in —so that React can detect prop equality properly and avoid unnecessary re-renders.

#react #typescript #frontend #hooks #eventhandling #reactjs
